advantages: Catching leakage early saves wasted deployment effort and prevents costly false confidence in model ROI.
Pipeline-first design (split → fit preprocessors on train → evaluate) yields metrics that track production more closely.
Explicit leakage audits force clearer definitions of prediction time and feature availability, improving product specs.
Time-aware validation surfaces realistic performance before capital or clinical decisions rely on the model.
disadvantages: Strict anti-leakage discipline can lower offline scores, which stakeholders may misread as regression rather than honesty.
Group and time splits reduce effective training size, increasing variance and sometimes requiring more data or simpler models.
Rebuilding feature stores to respect causality is engineering-heavy compared with quick notebook experiments.
Some legitimate proxies are subtle; exhaustive leakage review slows iteration for small teams.
